name: Vanilla Taffy
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease a large platter or jelly-roll pan.
2. In a medium-size heavy saucepan, combine sugar, corn syrup, water, and cream of tartar. Heat over medium heat, stirring constantly until boiling.
3. Continue to boil the mixture without stirring until it reaches 266°F on a candy thermometer (a small amount of syrup should form a hard ball when dropped into cold water). Remove from heat and stir in vanilla and butter or margarine.
4. Spread the mixture onto the prepared platter or pan. Allow to cool slightly until manageable.
5. Once cooled enough to handle, grease your hands with butter. Gather the mixture and pull it between your hands until it becomes smooth and light in color. Pull into large strips; twist off pieces.
6. Cut the taffy into 1-inch pieces using scissors. Store each piece individually wrapped in plastic wrap within an air-tight container.
Makes 1/2 pound.
From Family Circle 11/72 Homemade Candies
